knowledge.title: Pistol round: Armor vs full utility
knowledge.description: Buy kevlar + P250 + one nade as a default. Skip armor only if your role is pure entry on a fast rush where the first duel decides the round. Armor reduces damage from a P2000/USP body shot from 24 to 16 — enough to win one extra exchange per pistol round on average across a long sample.
knowledge.situation: Pistol round, deciding between armor and utility
knowledge.pro_example: Tier-1 meta: ~85% of CTs and ~70% of Ts buy armor on pistol when not committed to a 5-stack rush

knowledge.title: Anti-eco discipline: do not over-peek
knowledge.description: When the enemy is on a save (no rifles), the rifle side should NOT push aggressively into hold positions. Anti-eco losses are economy double-hits: you give them a free $3250 reward AND lose 1-2 rifles. Hold defaults at default angles, trade in 2s, and let them come to you.
knowledge.situation: Holding versus a force/eco round, full rifles
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: anti-eco round win rate drops below 70% when CTs over-rotate or stack one site

knowledge.title: Force buy: round 3 after pistol loss + lost conversion
knowledge.description: If you lose pistol AND lose the round-2 anti-eco (0-2), force buy with armor + Galil/FAMAS + a flash. Goal is NOT to win — it's to deal $3000+ damage and deny the enemy round-3 full buy. A force win flips momentum; a force loss costs you nothing because you were already broken.
knowledge.situation: Down 0-2, broke economy, deciding force vs save
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: round 3 force success ~30%, but enemy economy suppression converts ~55% of round 4s

knowledge.title: Save round discipline: no individual heroics
knowledge.description: On a save round, exit-frag from off-angles only if it costs zero risk. Do not push for ego frags. Saving even one rifle changes next round from a force buy to a half buy (rifle for one player), which raises the team's expected round-4 win probability by ~15%.
knowledge.situation: Save round, weapon retention priority
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: rifle retention rate on save rounds is ~65% for tier-1 teams, ~45% for tier-3

knowledge.title: Crosshair placement: head level, pre-aimed at angles
knowledge.description: Keep your crosshair at head height and pre-aimed at the next angle you will see. The single largest source of lost duels at intermediate skill is crosshair re-targeting time. If your crosshair is already on the angle when the enemy appears, your reaction time effectively drops by 100-150 ms.
knowledge.situation: Holding angles, clearing corners, low headshot percentage
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: top AWPers maintain head-level crosshair >95% of the time when moving through known sightlines

knowledge.title: Spray control: first 7 bullets are everything
knowledge.description: AK and M4 sprays are decisive in the first 7 bullets. Practice pulling down hard then slightly left for 3 bullets, then drift right. After 10 bullets, accuracy collapses — burst tap the rest. 90% of spray kills happen inside the first 7 bullets at <20m range.
knowledge.situation: Losing close-range rifle duels, low spray accuracy
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: tier-1 AK spray accuracy in first 7 bullets is ~80% at 10m

knowledge.title: Counter-strafe before every shot
knowledge.description: Tap the opposite movement key for one frame before shooting to instantly stop your model. Without counter-strafe your first bullet has up to 10× the inaccuracy spread. This is the single highest-leverage mechanical habit at intermediate skill levels.
knowledge.situation: Missing first shots while peeking, low first-bullet accuracy
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: 100% of tier-1 players counter-strafe; the habit is automatic after a few months of practice

knowledge.title: Trade kills > survival in 2v2 retakes
knowledge.description: In a retake, the second player's job is to trade the first player's death within 2 seconds, NOT to stay alive. A traded death turns a 1v2 into a 1v1 with bomb time pressure on the enemy. Untraded deaths bleed retake win rate from ~50% to ~15%.
knowledge.situation: CT retake, deciding whether to push together or stagger
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: 2v2 retakes with successful trade win ~52%, untraded retakes win ~17%

knowledge.title: Mid-round calling: information triggers, not impulses
knowledge.description: Mid-round calls should be based on hard information triggers — confirmed enemy positions, utility usage, or timing reads — not gut feelings. Define your team's triggers before the round (e.g., 'no smoke on connector by 0:50 = rotate B'). Impulse rotates leak round win rate.
knowledge.situation: IGL deciding to rotate or commit mid-round
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: tier-1 IGLs make ~3 mid-round calls per round, ~80% based on a confirmed trigger

knowledge.title: Default setup: 2-1-2 by default, adapt by buy
knowledge.description: On a default round with full buys both sides, the standard CT setup is 2 A, 1 mid/connector, 2 B. Adapt by economy: if Ts are on a force, stack the most valuable site (the one they typically hit on forces). If CTs are on a force, flatten utility on chokes and play for time.
knowledge.situation: CT default setup, deciding spread
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: 2-1-2 is the most common CT base on 6 of 7 active duty maps

knowledge.title: Damage callouts: number, location, weapon
knowledge.description: Always call damage as 'NN damage, location, weapon' — e.g. '85 in 2, long, AK'. Teammates use this to make immediate decisions: a 15 HP enemy is a free trade target. Generic 'one shot' callouts waste 1-2 seconds of decision-making time per engagement.
knowledge.situation: Team coordination after a damage trade
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: tier-1 IGL teams make damage callouts on >90% of confirmed hits

knowledge.title: Utility economy: do not waste smokes on contact
knowledge.description: Smokes are the highest-value utility in the buy menu ($300 each). Throwing a smoke after first contact, where it cuts no rotation and isolates no angle, is a wasted $300. Save smokes for execute timings, post-plant cutoffs, or pre-fire denial of common sightlines.
knowledge.situation: Mid-round, deciding whether to throw a smoke reactively
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: average smoke cost per kill is ~$120 for tier-1, ~$210 for tier-3 — efficiency matters

knowledge.title: Post-plant: hold the angle the bomb plant favors
knowledge.description: After plant, your job is NOT to hunt — it's to hold the angle that forces the CT to defuse from the worst possible position. On most plants there is one 'lurk angle' where you can pre-fire the defuser. Find it for each common plant spot and rehearse it.
knowledge.situation: T post-plant, deciding where to hold
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: ~70% of post-plant T wins come from a single pre-aimed lurk angle

knowledge.title: Bomb plant placement: maximize defuse exposure
knowledge.description: Always plant where the defuser is exposed to your hold angle, not where it's fastest to plant. A 'safe plant' that lets the CT defuse behind cover is an automatic round loss in 2v3 and worse situations. The 1-second extra plant time is worth it.
knowledge.situation: T executing, deciding plant spot
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: optimal plant spots improve post-plant win rate by 8-12 percentage points

knowledge.title: 1vX clutch: separate them with time, not space
knowledge.description: In a clutch, the goal is to create time gaps between the enemies — not necessarily distance. Use a corner that forces them to cross a chokepoint one at a time. Sound discipline (walk!) and isolation are worth more than aim in 1v3+.
knowledge.situation: 1vX clutch scenario, low clutch win rate
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: 1v2 clutch win rate is ~22% for tier-1, dropping to ~8% in 1v3

knowledge.title: AWPer positioning: one shot, then reposition
knowledge.description: After firing your AWP, immediately reposition. The enemy team now knows your angle and will throw utility or peek with numbers. Holding the same angle for a second shot is the #1 cause of AWPer deaths at intermediate skill.
knowledge.situation: Playing AWP, dying after the second peek
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: top AWPers reposition within 3 seconds of firing >85% of the time

knowledge.title: Movement: walk at every information-sensitive moment
knowledge.description: Walking is silent. Running is broadcast. Walk in the last 5 meters of any rotation, in any post-plant approach, and any time you do not want to give a free position read. The 1-second walk window is worth more than the 1-second time save of running.
knowledge.situation: Rotating or repositioning, getting picked off
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: tier-1 players walk for ~30% of round time, vs ~10% for tier-3

knowledge.title: Utility usage timing: stagger, do not stack
knowledge.description: When executing a site, stagger your utility over 4-6 seconds: smoke first, then molly, then flash on entry. Stacking all utility in the same second wastes the cover window and gives the CT a single 'wait' moment instead of three decision points.
knowledge.situation: T execute, utility stacking up
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: optimal utility stagger improves entry frag rate by ~12%

knowledge.title: Map control: slow defaults beat fast hits on equal economy
knowledge.description: When both teams are full buy, slow defaults (60-80 second build-up with map control) outperform fast hits by ~8 percentage points. Fast hits work as a punish or against overstacks — not as a default strategy.
knowledge.situation: T-side, full buy, choosing pace
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: tier-1 teams use fast hits on ~15% of full-buy rounds; the rest are slow defaults

knowledge.title: Smoke timing: throw before you need cover, not after
knowledge.description: Smokes have a 1.5s deploy time — they only block vision after fully expanding. Throwing a smoke as you're already in the open is too late; you'll die before it covers you. Pre-throw smokes 2 seconds before you need them.
knowledge.situation: Dying while smoke is still expanding
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: optimal smoke deploys land 2.0-2.5s before the cover is needed

knowledge.title: Trading discipline: stand close enough to get the trade
knowledge.description: If you're more than ~5 meters from your teammate when they peek, you cannot trade them in time. Common error: entry fragger pushes 10m ahead while support 'covers' from too far back. Stay tight on entries.
knowledge.situation: Entry fraggers dying without trades
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: average trade distance for successful trades is ~4m, ~7m for failed trades

knowledge.title: Eco round exit fragging: one player, one angle
knowledge.description: On an eco, send one player to a single off-angle for an exit frag. Sending multiple players for ego frags loses 2-3 weapons that could have been saved. Pro teams treat eco rounds as economy management, not gambling.
knowledge.situation: Eco round, deciding exit frag commitment
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: tier-1 eco round exit frag commitment is ~1 player on ~60% of ecos

knowledge.title: Bomb time management: 35-second plant rule
knowledge.description: Plant the bomb before 0:35 remaining. Earlier plants give you a full 40-second post-plant where the CT must come to you. Plants after 0:35 leave too little time for the bomb to tick down — you may have to defend a retake under time pressure.
knowledge.situation: T execute timing, slow plants
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: post-plant win rate drops ~15% for plants after 0:30

knowledge.title: CT rotation: rotate on confirmation, not suspicion
knowledge.description: Do not rotate off your site based on a single sound cue. Rotate when at least two cues confirm the enemy commitment (e.g., utility usage + footsteps + damage on a teammate). False rotates leak ~20% of CT rounds at intermediate skill.
knowledge.situation: CT-side, deciding to rotate
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: tier-1 CT rotation accuracy (correct rotates / total rotates) is >85%

knowledge.title: First contact economics: do not commit utility on a pick
knowledge.description: When your AWPer takes a pick, do NOT immediately follow up with smokes/flashes. The pick has already created the advantage; spending utility doubles down on a round you've already won. Save the utility for the next site execute.
knowledge.situation: After a successful opening pick
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: tier-1 teams use ~30% less utility on rounds with a successful opening pick
